methods.node_size: Degree-based only; no stars, citations, or popularity values are inferred.
methods.inferred_edges: Deterministic shared-context similarity with score >= 3.8 and at most three inferred edges per node.
methods.layout: Deterministic build-time positions for ecosystem, first-declared-stage closed loop, and public-year timeline modes.

nodes.name: Perspectives for self-driving labs in synthetic biology
nodes.description: A biology-specific perspective on how self-driving laboratories could reshape synthetic-biology design–build–test–learn cycles.

nodes.name: Autonomous ‘self-driving’ laboratories: a review of technology and policy implications
nodes.description: A recent review that separates self-driving-lab technologies from their governance, policy, and societal implications.

nodes.name: Robot Scientist Adam / The Automation of Science
nodes.description: A landmark robot scientist that generated and tested functional-genomics hypotheses in yeast.

nodes.name: BacterAI
nodes.description: An automated platform that turns microbial-metabolism questions into robot-executed experiments and iteratively learns interpretable rules.

nodes.name: Generalized AI-powered autonomous enzyme engineering platform
nodes.description: A generalized autonomous enzyme-engineering workflow linking AI-guided design, worklist generation, robotic execution, assays, and iterative learning.

nodes.name: LUMI-lab
nodes.description: A foundation-model-driven autonomous laboratory for iterative discovery of ionizable lipids for mRNA delivery.

nodes.name: BioDiscoveryAgent
nodes.description: A tool-using LLM agent for proposing, executing computational analyses, and refining biological discovery workflows.

nodes.name: Robin
nodes.description: A multi-agent system that automates literature research, hypothesis generation, and computational analysis across a scientific-discovery campaign.

nodes.name: Biomni
nodes.description: A general-purpose biomedical agent environment that plans analyses and calls a broad collection of biological tools and databases.

nodes.name: LaMBO
nodes.description: A latent-space Bayesian-optimization method for multi-objective biological sequence design.

nodes.name: BRADSHAW
nodes.description: A closed-loop Bayesian optimization framework for designing informative experiments under small-data constraints.

nodes.name: GEARS
nodes.description: A graph-based model for predicting transcriptional responses to unseen genetic perturbations.

nodes.name: Compositional Perturbation Autoencoder (CPA)
nodes.description: A compositional model for predicting single-cell responses to unseen combinations of perturbations, doses, and covariates.

nodes.name: EVOLVEpro
nodes.description: A protein-engineering framework that combines sequence models with iterative experimental feedback to prioritize variants.

nodes.name: Low-N protein engineering
nodes.description: A practical small-data machine-learning workflow for prioritizing protein variants with limited measurements.

nodes.name: Machine-learning-assisted directed protein evolution (MLDE)
nodes.description: A machine-learning-guided strategy for selecting compact, informative variant libraries for directed evolution.

nodes.name: Robot Scientist Eve
nodes.description: A robot scientist that automated phenotypic screening and identified candidate compounds for neglected-disease drug discovery.

nodes.name: Quadratic Phenotypic Optimization Platform (QPOP)
nodes.description: A phenotype-driven platform for efficiently searching and optimizing multidrug combinations.

nodes.name: NSF iBioFoundry (iBioFAB)
nodes.description: A highly automated synthetic-biology biofoundry that provides infrastructure for design–build–test–learn workflows.

nodes.name: eVOLVER
nodes.description: A scalable continuous-culture platform supporting programmable, feedback-controlled microbial experiments.

nodes.name: Autoprotocol
nodes.description: A machine-readable protocol specification and software ecosystem for describing executable laboratory procedures.

nodes.name: LabOP
nodes.description: An ontology-based language for representing, exchanging, and compiling laboratory protocols across execution environments.

nodes.name: Aquarium
nodes.description: A laboratory operating system for planning, executing, tracking, and reproducing complex biological workflows.

nodes.name: HELAO
nodes.description: A modular orchestration framework for coordinating instrument servers and automated laboratory experiments.

nodes.name: MADSci
nodes.description: A modular framework for scheduling workflows and coordinating devices, resources, and data in autonomous laboratories.

nodes.name: PyLabRobot
nodes.description: A hardware-agnostic Python framework for controlling liquid handlers and other laboratory devices.

nodes.name: SiLA 2
nodes.description: A standard and software base for interoperable communication with laboratory instruments.

nodes.name: CellProfiler
nodes.description: A widely used open platform for reproducible, high-throughput quantitative analysis of biological images.

nodes.name: DeepCell Kiosk
nodes.description: A deployable deep-learning system that makes cell-image analysis accessible as an operational measurement service.

nodes.name: Automated Recommendation Tool (ART)
nodes.description: A Bayesian ensemble framework that learns from biological experiments and recommends new designs under uncertainty.

nodes.name: Active Learning-assisted Directed Evolution (ALDE)
nodes.description: An iterative active-learning workflow that updates sequence–fitness models across rounds of directed-evolution experiments.

nodes.name: ISA-Tab and ISA tools
nodes.description: A standards and tooling ecosystem for structuring investigation, study, assay, and provenance metadata in life science.

nodes.name: Allotrope Framework and Allotrope Data Format
nodes.description: A framework and data format for standardizing analytical laboratory data, metadata, and semantics.

nodes.name: Vivarium
nodes.description: A framework for composing heterogeneous biological models into multiscale simulations.

nodes.name: BioSimulators
nodes.description: A registry and standardized execution ecosystem for reproducible simulation of biological models.

nodes.name: LABBench2
nodes.description: A benchmark suite for evaluating AI systems on nearly 1,900 biology-research tasks across reasoning and laboratory-relevant skills.

nodes.name: Virtual Cell Challenge
nodes.description: A community challenge and dataset ecosystem for evaluating models that predict cellular responses to perturbations.

nodes.name: ProteinGym
nodes.description: A large evaluation suite for protein-sequence models using experimentally measured fitness landscapes.

nodes.name: BacterAI autonomous-experiment dataset
nodes.description: Published data from BacterAI’s iterative microbial-metabolism experiments, suitable for studying active-learning trajectories.

nodes.name: LUMI-lab iterative LNP design data
nodes.description: Data and mapping tables from iterative ionizable-lipid design and testing in LUMI-lab.

nodes.name: ToolUniverse
nodes.description: A large scientific tool ecosystem that exposes more than a thousand research resources for agentic workflows.

nodes.name: BioMCP
nodes.description: An MCP and command-line interface that gives agents structured access to trusted biomedical sources.

nodes.name: Chi.Bio
nodes.description: An open-source, networked bioreactor platform for automated continuous-culture experiments.

nodes.name: OpenFlexure Microscope
nodes.description: An open, motorized microscope platform that can serve as an imaging and measurement endpoint in automated biology workflows.

nodes.name: Opentrons OT-2
nodes.description: A programmable liquid-handling robot with official hardware files and an open software stack used across biological automation.

nodes.name: Emerald Cloud Lab
nodes.description: A commercial remote laboratory where users specify and run experiments through a cloud software interface.

nodes.name: Culture Biosciences
nodes.description: A commercial cloud-connected bioreactor platform for remote bioprocess development and data collection.

nodes.name: Arctoris Ulysses
nodes.description: A commercial robotic drug-discovery laboratory platform centered on its Ulysses automation stack.

nodes.name: Global Biofoundries Alliance
nodes.description: An international alliance connecting biofoundries and promoting shared capabilities, standards, and collaboration.

nodes.name: Lab Automation Forums
nodes.description: A practitioner community for laboratory automation, integration, troubleshooting, and shared implementation knowledge.

nodes.name: Building a biofoundry
nodes.description: A practical, biology-specific guide to the capabilities, organization, workflows, and challenges involved in building a biofoundry.

nodes.name: Frontiers in biofoundry: opportunities and challenges
nodes.description: A recent perspective on biofoundry capabilities, adoption barriers, standards, and opportunities for automated biological engineering.

nodes.name: BioAutomata
nodes.description: A fully automated design–build–test–learn platform that paired Bayesian experiment selection with iBioFAB execution to optimize lycopene biosynthesis.

nodes.name: SAMPLE
nodes.description: Self-driving Autonomous Machines for Protein Landscape Exploration integrates an intelligent agent with robotic gene synthesis, expression, and activity measurement.

nodes.name: PLMeAE
nodes.description: A protein-language-model-enabled automatic evolution platform connecting mutant design with biofoundry build-and-test operations.

nodes.name: The Virtual Lab
nodes.description: A multi-agent scientific team that generated and debated nanobody design hypotheses, with selected designs subsequently tested experimentally.

nodes.name: CRISPR-GPT
nodes.description: An LLM-based agent that assists researchers with CRISPR design choices, reagent selection, protocol planning, and downstream analysis.

nodes.name: CellAgent
nodes.description: A multi-agent framework that plans and executes computational single-cell and spatial-omics analyses.

nodes.name: METIS
nodes.description: An active-learning framework for efficiently selecting informative biological experiments and optimizing complex cell-free systems.

nodes.name: Bayesian optimization for iterative cell-culture media development
nodes.description: An iterative Bayesian-optimization workflow for accelerating cell-culture media development, including PBMC maintenance and process applications.

nodes.name: scGen
nodes.description: A generative single-cell model for predicting cell-type-specific responses to perturbations across conditions.

nodes.name: CellOT
nodes.description: A neural optimal-transport model that predicts how single-cell populations change under perturbation.

nodes.name: CellOracle
nodes.description: A single-cell framework for reconstructing gene-regulatory networks and simulating transcription-factor perturbations.

nodes.name: MULTI-evolve
nodes.description: A machine-learning-guided framework for rapidly identifying high-performing multi-mutant proteins from compact experimental measurements.

nodes.name: MODIFY
nodes.description: A machine-learning method for designing diverse, evolutionarily plausible protein libraries while co-optimizing predicted fitness.

nodes.name: Machine-learning-guided cell-free enzyme engineering
nodes.description: A high-throughput platform integrating cell-free DNA assembly, expression, functional assays, and machine-learning-guided enzyme design.

nodes.name: DrugReflector
nodes.description: An active-learning framework that uses transcriptomic signatures and wet-lab feedback to prioritize compounds for phenotypic drug discovery.

nodes.name: BATCHIE
nodes.description: A Bayesian active-learning platform that dynamically selects batches for large drug-combination screens and updates from observed results.

nodes.name: Agile BioFoundry
nodes.description: A U.S. national-laboratory consortium providing integrated design–build–test–learn capabilities for biomanufacturing.

nodes.name: DAMP Lab
nodes.description: Boston University's Design, Automation, Manufacturing, and Processes facility for high-throughput synthetic-biology workflows.

nodes.name: London Biofoundry
nodes.description: A biofoundry providing automated biological design, construction, testing, and characterization capabilities.

nodes.name: BioCoder
nodes.description: An early programming language for expressing biology protocols in a standardized, machine-actionable form.

nodes.name: BioPlanner
nodes.description: A protocol-planning evaluation framework and BioProt dataset that map biological protocols to pseudocode representations.

nodes.name: Antha
nodes.description: A commercial laboratory software platform for composing, executing, and tracking automated biological workflows.

nodes.name: Riffyn X
nodes.description: A commercial platform for structuring experimental processes, connecting data and materials, and preserving R&D provenance.

nodes.name: PyHamilton
nodes.description: An open Python interface for scripting and coordinating Hamilton liquid-handling robots.

nodes.name: Micro-Manager
nodes.description: A widely used open platform for microscope hardware control, automated acquisition, and imaging workflows.

nodes.name: MCMICRO
nodes.description: A modular, reproducible computational pipeline for processing highly multiplexed tissue images into analysis-ready spatial data.

nodes.name: DeepCell
nodes.description: An open deep-learning library and ecosystem for cellular image segmentation and quantitative analysis.

nodes.name: OPEX
nodes.description: An optimal experimental-design method that uses active learning to select informative omics experiments and improve models with fewer measurements.

nodes.name: Active-learning-guided optimization of cell-free biosensors
nodes.description: A multi-objective active-learning workflow that iteratively engineered cell-free transcription-factor biosensors for sensitive and selective lead detection.

nodes.name: Synthetic Biology Open Language Version 3 (SBOL 3)
nodes.description: An open standard for representing the structure, function, and design intent of engineered biological systems.

nodes.name: OME-NGFF
nodes.description: An open, cloud-native specification for storing and interoperably accessing large multidimensional bioimaging data.

nodes.name: COBRApy
nodes.description: A widely used open Python library for constraint-based reconstruction and simulation of metabolic networks.

nodes.name: CellModeller
nodes.description: An open framework for individual-based modeling of multicellular microbial systems, growth, mechanics, and genetic circuits.

nodes.name: FLIP
nodes.description: A benchmark suite with realistic data splits for evaluating machine-learning models on protein fitness landscapes.

nodes.name: Therapeutics Data Commons
nodes.description: An open ecosystem of AI-ready therapeutic datasets, tasks, data splits, evaluators, and leaderboards.

nodes.name: scPerturBench
nodes.description: A reproducible benchmark of methods for generalizable single-cell perturbation-response prediction across diverse datasets and scenarios.

nodes.name: SAMPLE autonomous protein-engineering campaign data
nodes.description: Publication-linked measurements and design history from SAMPLE's autonomous protein-landscape exploration campaigns.

nodes.name: BATCHIE prospective combination-screen data
nodes.description: Prospective sequential drug-combination measurements associated with BATCHIE's pediatric-cancer screening campaign.

nodes.name: BioChatter
nodes.description: An open framework for building reproducible conversational-AI applications grounded in biomedical tools and knowledge graphs.

nodes.name: BioThings Explorer
nodes.description: A query engine that dynamically chains semantically annotated biomedical APIs into a federated knowledge graph.

nodes.name: gget
nodes.description: A command-line and Python toolkit that provides programmatic access to genomic reference databases and common analysis services.

nodes.name: OpenDrop digital microfluidics
nodes.description: An open digital-microfluidics platform for programming droplet operations and developing personal bioautomation applications.

nodes.name: Poseidon syringe-pump and microscope system
nodes.description: A low-cost open system combining programmable syringe pumps and microscopy for microfluidic laboratory experiments.

nodes.name: Strateos cloud laboratory
nodes.description: A commercial robotic cloud-laboratory platform for remotely specifying and running life-science experiments.

nodes.name: LabGenius EVA
nodes.description: A proprietary machine-learning and automated-experimentation platform for iterative discovery and optimization of multispecific antibodies.

nodes.name: Ginkgo Bioworks autonomous labs and foundry platform
nodes.description: A commercial biological-engineering foundry offering automated and increasingly autonomous laboratory capabilities as a service.

nodes.name: Society for Laboratory Automation and Screening (SLAS)
nodes.description: A professional society and conference ecosystem for laboratory automation, screening, instrumentation, and applied life-science technology.

nodes.name: International Workshop on Bio-Design Automation (IWBDA)
nodes.description: A long-running workshop community connecting synthetic biology with design automation, standards, tools, and laboratory execution.
